About Dr. Pendley

The Physician Behind
In Good Health

Dr. Bruce Pendley has spent more than three decades doing what he loves most: caring for patients and families across every stage of life. As the Medical Director of In Good Health, he brings the kind of steady, experienced leadership that gives both patients and clinical staff confidence that they are in good hands.

His academic foundation is exceptional. He earned his Chemistry degree from Emory University in Atlanta in 1983—a rigorous scientific grounding that would define his evidence-based approach to medicine—before going on to complete his MD at the Medical College of Georgia in Augusta in 1987.

Following medical school, Dr. Pendley completed his Family Practice Residency in Rome, Georgia (1987–1990), emerging as a board-certified physician through the American Academy of Family Physicians (AAFP). His specialty training in Hospice and Palliative Care gave him a profound perspective on the human side of medicine.

Today, Dr. Pendley works closely alongside Dr. Laurie Davis, NP, who leads day-to-day patient care. Together, under the umbrella of AlwaysInGoodHealth.com, they offer patients a seamless experience grounded in trust, expertise, and genuine compassion.

Outside of medicine, Dr. Pendley is a devoted husband to Debbie—the love of his life—and a proud father. His focus on health is deeply personal: he wants to be the greatest positive force in his family’s wellbeing so he can enjoy every moment with them.

Leadership

Guiding In Good Health
with Vision & Integrity

As Medical Director, Dr. Pendley sets the clinical standard for everything In Good Health does. His oversight ensures that every patient receives care that meets the highest professional and ethical standards.

“Better health for the whole family isn’t just a tagline. It’s a commitment we take seriously with every single patient.”

Dr. Pendley’s leadership model is rooted in collaboration, trust, and the belief that the best care happens when physician and patient truly know one another.

Clinical Standards Oversight
Dr. Pendley’s MD oversight ensures all protocols, prescriptions, and care plans meet the highest standards of safety and efficacy.
Whole-Family Philosophy
With deep roots in Family Practice, Dr. Pendley built In Good Health to serve patients across every stage of life.
Decades of Earned Trust
35+ years of practice means patients benefit from a physician who has truly seen it all—and who leads with calm, informed confidence.
Compassionate Palliative Care Roots
Specialty training in Hospice & Palliative Care instilled a human-first approach that permeates every aspect of In Good Health.
Our Approach

Medicine the Way It
Should Be

01
Science-First Foundation
A Chemistry degree from Emory and an MD from MCG means Dr. Pendley approaches every patient with rigorous scientific thinking.
02
Listening Before Treating
Decades in Family Practice taught Dr. Pendley that the most powerful diagnostic tool is a physician who truly listens.
03
End-to-End Life Care
From preventive wellness to Hospice and Palliative support, In Good Health walks alongside patients at every stage.
04
Collaborative Provider Model
Dr. Pendley and Dr. Laurie Davis, NP work as a true team—combining medical oversight with hands-on, relationship-centered care.
05
Family-Rooted Values
From devoted family man to beekeeper, Dr. Pendley lives the values he practices: patience, stewardship, service, and love.
06
Accessible Expert Guidance
Through Dr. Davis, NP, In Good Health offers expert, personalized consultations for every patient. Book at DrDavisNP.com →
